behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is about whether the real model produces the correct signature from natural language. A mocked model can validate parsing/validation logic, but it cannot expose generation-quality or variability failures in signature formation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real LLM with a prompt that specifies an exact typed signature; assert the produced function signature matches required parameter names, parameter types, and return type. Repeat enough runs/prompts to detect model-variance mismatches.

behaviors.description: The generated implementation passes all provided test cases on the first or a subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is orchestration with real code execution (test harness/subprocess/files/runtime). You can stub the LLM and still catch real failures in compile/run/test wiring and retry progression. Agentic quality is relevant but not required to validate this behavior’s pass-on-attempt-N contract.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic LLM stubs that return a failing implementation first and a passing implementation second; execute the real test runner path (same subprocess/runtime used in production) and assert the service reports success on attempt 2 with correct attempt history.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement step includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from its mistakes.
behaviors.justification: This is a module handoff bug class: test-runner output must flow into prompt-construction for the next attempt. A single-function unit test is often too narrow unless prompt assembly is fully isolated; no real OS/LLM call is required to catch the boundary failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local modules for attempt orchestration + prompt builder with a fake failing test result payload; intercept the second-attempt LLM request and assert it contains the exact failure messages (or required normalized excerpts).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears in retry-loop state management plus result aggregation across attempts. Deterministic stubs are sufficient; real network/model calls are unnecessary for this specific contract.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with stubs that force test failure on every attempt; assert exactly 3 attempts are executed, no 4th call is made, and returned error includes per-attempt artifacts/results in the required schema.

behaviors.description: The full loop — generate, test, refine, test again — produces a working implementation for a function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (initial generation then refinement informed by failures). The target failure mode is end-to-end agent pipeline effectiveness, not just wiring or single-call output.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real LLM and real test execution on a benchmark prompt known to usually fail in one-shot mode; assert the multi-attempt loop converges to passing tests within 3 attempts and captures intermediate failure-to-refinement transitions.
